verbatim_text: Great. So the normal recipe of using 20 tokens for parameter is out the window. This new foundation model, this LFM model was trained at roughly 80,000 tokens per parameter. And as you increase your new budget, that ratio might even increase. So industry is already over-training these small reasoning models, but they're doing so in a very ad hoc way. To our knowledge, they don't have any kind of like scaling law or math or understanding of how this universe of these parameters interact with one another. And so they're kind of just, you know, deciding, okay, these models are small, we need small models to do inference in these agentic harnesses much more efficiently. And so our best bet is to over-train them. And so that's kind of the rationale within industry. And this work establishes the first actual scientific law for how to do this. And so we've given industry now a map for how to produce these small reasoning models. Great.
